Decision on Protective measures

Ordonnance relative aux mesures de protection

We You Bunleng and Marcel Lemonde Co Investigating Judges of the Extraordinary Chambers in
the Courts of Cambodia

Having seen the Law on the Establishment of the Extraordinary Chambers dated October 27 2004

Having seen Rules 29 and 55 of the Internal Rules of the Extraordinary Chambers

Having seen the judicial investigation opened against KAING GUECK EAV alias DUCH

Having seen the request for protective measures submitted by the Co Prosecutors within the
Introductory Submission dated July 18 2007 paragraphs 119 120 121 and 125

REASONS FOR THE DECISION

The request demands that protective measures be ordered with respect to victims witnesses and third
persons who are referred to
in this Introductory
Submission the accompanying case file and any other material provided therewith
The Co
Prosecutors consider that these measures should include in particular the prohibition of defence
access to the list of potential witnesses
until such time as a comprehensive specific individual
assessment is undertaken

either directly or by necessary implication

The request does not specify the names of the individuals concerned nor how the failure to provide
protective measures would endanger the lives and security of the hundreds of victims witnesses or
third persons mentioned in the Introductory Submission and in its annexes 14000 pages Moreover
it does not explain the reasons why an analysis of the real risks entailed by some of these individuals
was not done during the long preliminary inquiry preceding the opening of the judicial investigation

It is clear that the Co Investigating Judges must take all appropriate measures to ensure the protection
of witnesses and victims However they must not do so indiscriminately
that is to say they cannot
do so without a clear justification and for an unlimited number of individuals They must also
guarantee the rights of the defence which are fundamental rights in a criminal trial and which include

 in particular the right of the lawyers for the Charged Person to have access to the case file and
annexed documents

the request as drafted cannot be accepted However in the current
Given these considerations
absence of any effective measures and during the period needed to consult the units responsible for the
protection of witnesses and victims in order to determine whether it may be necessary to order
protective measures for certain victims and witnesses the delivery of copies of the case file to the
lawyers for the parties must be denied In addition they must be reminded that they are bound by
professional secrecy and must not divulge the contents of any information obtained from consulting the
case file to any person in any manner whatsoever in particular the names and addresses of the
witnesses under penalty of disciplinary and criminal sanctions

FOR THESE REASONS

1

2

3

4

5

6

Partially deny the request for protective measures dated July 18 2007
Allow the lawyers for the Charged Person to consult the entire case file and its annexed
documents for the preparation of his defence
Prohibit provisionally and until completion of a detailed evaluation of the risk incurred by all
individuals mentioned in the Introductory Submission and the annexed documents the delivery
of copies of the case file to the lawyers for the parties
Recall that everyone who has access to the case file is prohibited from disclosing publicly in
any manner whatsoever any information obtained through consultation of the case file
especially the names and relevant information that would allow the identification and location
of the witnesses under penalty of disciplinary or criminal sanctions in accordance with Rules
35 and 38 of the Internal Rules of the Extraordinary Chambers
State that for the purposes of this order the term public means any physical person or legal
entity government organization entity group and media and that it includes in particular the
family friends and relatives of the Charged Person moreover the term
and
annexed documents means any information statements minutes reports photos or other
items submitted whether in paper or electronic format
State that the present order does not preclude any party or person from requesting
in
accordance with Rule 29 of the Internal Rules any protective measures they deem to be
necessary but that in support of such request they must specify

case file

a

the identity of the witnesses victims or third persons who would benefit from these
measures and

b how in the absence of the measures the lives and health of these witnesses victims

their families or their kin would be placed in grave danger

This order was drafted in Khmer and French and subsequently translated into English

Done in Phnom Penh July 30 2007

Co Investigating Judges
